reasoning: Based on estimates and reports as of late October 2025, 2025 is unlikely to surpass 2024 as the hottest year on record in the NASA GISTEMP dataset. While 2025 started exceptionally strong (with January being a record warm month), major climate monitoring organizations and analysts (including Carbon Brief and NOAA/Weather.com) projected mid-year that 2025 would likely finish as the 2nd or 3rd warmest year. This trend is driven by the transition from the strong El Niño conditions that boosted early 2024 to early 2025 into ENSO-neutral and eventually La Niña conditions, which typically exert a cooling influence on global surface temperatures in the latter half of the year. Historically, the year an El Niño ends (like 2024) is often a record-setter, while the following year (2025) starts very warm but cools relatively as La Niña develops. Given the consensus and the physical drivers, the probability of 2025 beating the 2024 NASA GISTEMP record is low.
